mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate with stable CPIAUCSL implies an easing discount-rate channel that lifts equity valuations immediately, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive more often after 1-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent path signals worsening income risk and weaker aggregate demand, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if sign accuracy falls below 50% in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand stabilization and inventory absorption, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in retail sales and housing starts do not precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect fully in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while PAYEMS remains weak, output is recovering without labor reabsorption, a pattern that usually preserves margin pressure and keeps S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS lags by at least 0.2% over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-up/PAYEMS-lag regime is followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction term is not significant out of sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in GDPC1 reflects a broad demand recession that reduces expected earnings and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if negative quarterly GDPC1 growth does not align with lower 60d SPX returns, or if the relationship flips sign in recession windows.

mechanism_narrative: High CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a low FEDFUNDS rate indicates policy is behind the inflation shock, so real rates stay negative and equity multiples compress, leading to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate episodes do not produce negative 20d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL is controlled independently.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent PAYEMS declines alongside elevated UNRATE capture a payroll shock that weakens household income expectations and should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE is above 10%
failure_condition: Reject if payroll contraction plus elevated unemployment fails to predict negative 5d SPX returns, or if returns are positive in most stress-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rebound in HOUST after a prior collapse signals reopening-sensitive demand normalization and improving financing conditions, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebound episodes do not precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the signal vanishes when FEDFUNDS is unchanged.
